question: tell me about sexual division of labour.
answer:
The sexual division of labor refers to the societal norm or pattern in which different tasks, roles, or jobs are assigned disproportionately along gender lines. Globally, this division is observed across various cultures and has historically been characterized by the breadwinner-homemaker model in many societies.

In a general sense, tasks related to work outside, dangerous work, and highly technical disciplines, such as those in STEM fields (with the exception of health care), are more likely to be performed by men. Conversely, work associated with care and interpersonal relations is often the origin of the sexual division of labor.
